label: Apple Scab
description: Apple scab is a serious disease of apples and ornamental crabapples caused by Venturia inaequalis that attacks both leaves and fruit. The fungal disease forms pale yellow or olive-green spots on the upper surface of leaves.

label: Apple Black rot
description: Black rot is a disease of apples that infects fruit, leaves and bark caused by the fungus Botryosphaeria obtusa. It can also jump to healthy tissue on pear or quince trees, but is typically a secondary fungus of weak or dead tissues in other plants.

label: Apple Cedar rust
description: Cedar apple rust is a fungal disease caused by Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae that requires juniper plants to complete its complicated two year life-cycle. Spores overwinter as a reddish-brown gall on young twigs of various juniper species.

label: Apple healthy
description: No disease

label: Blueberry healthy
description: No disease

label: Cherry(Sour) Mildew
description: Powdery mildew of sweet and sour cherry is caused by Podosphaera clandestina, an obligate biotrophic fungus. Mid- and late-season sweet cherry (Prunus avium) cultivars are commonly affected, rendering them unmarketable due to the covering of white fungal growth on the cherry surface.

label: Cherry(Sour) healthy
description: No disease

label: Corn Leaf spot
description: Northern corn leaf spot mainly occurs on inbred lines used in seed production. The disease is rarely a problem on hybrids. Symptoms of this disease are sometimes confused with northern corn leaf blight, southern corn leaf blight, and anthracnose.

label: Corn Common rust
description: [DRAFT] Corn common rust is caused by the fungus Puccinia sorghi. It produces small, oval to elongate cinnamon-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces, which can reduce photosynthetic area and yield in susceptible hybrids under cool, humid conditions.

label: Corn Northern Leaf Blight
description: [DRAFT] Northern corn leaf blight is caused by the fungus Exserohilum turcicum. It produces long, cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions on leaves that can coalesce and blight large areas of foliage in warm, humid weather.

label: Corn healthy
description: No disease

label: Grape Black rot
description: [DRAFT] Grape black rot is caused by the fungus Guignardia bidwellii. It appears as small reddish-brown spots with dark borders on leaves and shrivels infected berries into hard, black 'mummies'.

label: Grape Black measles
description: [DRAFT] Also known as Esca, this is a complex grapevine trunk disease caused by several wood-rotting fungi. It produces tiger-stripe interveinal chlorosis and necrosis on leaves and can cause sudden vine collapse ('apoplexy').

label: Grape Leaf Blight
description: [DRAFT] Also known as Isariopsis leaf spot, this disease is caused by the fungus Pseudocercospora vitis. It produces irregular reddish-brown spots on leaves that can merge and cause premature defoliation.

label: Grape healthy
description: No disease

label: Citrus greening
description: [DRAFT] Citrus greening, or Huanglongbing, is a bacterial disease caused by Candidatus Liberibacter species and spread by the Asian citrus psyllid. It causes blotchy, mottled leaves, misshapen bitter fruit, and eventual tree decline; there is currently no cure.

label: Peach Bacterial spot
description: [DRAFT] Bacterial spot of peach is caused by Xanthomonas arboricola pv. pruni. It produces small, dark, angular lesions on leaves that may fall out leaving a 'shot-hole' appearance, along with sunken, pitted spots on fruit.

label: Peach healthy
description: No disease

label: Bell_Pepper Bacterial spot
description: [DRAFT] Bacterial spot of pepper is caused by Xanthomonas species. It produces small, water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown with yellow halos, and raised, scab-like spots on fruit.

label: Bell_Pepper healthy
description: No disease

label: Potato Early Blight
description: [DRAFT] Early blight of potato is caused by the fungus Alternaria solani. It produces dark brown lesions with concentric rings, giving a 'target spot' pattern, usually starting on older leaves.

label: Potato Late Blight
description: [DRAFT] Late blight of potato is caused by the oomycete Phytophthora infestans, the pathogen behind the Irish potato famine. It produces water-soaked lesions with white fuzzy growth on the underside of leaves in humid conditions, and can kill foliage rapidly.

label: Potato healthy
description: No disease

label: Raspberry healthy
description: No disease

label: Soybean healthy
description: No disease

label: Squash Powdery mildew
description: [DRAFT] Powdery mildew of squash is caused by fungi such as Podosphaera xanthii and Erysiphe cichoracearum. It appears as white, powdery fungal growth on leaf surfaces that reduces photosynthesis and yield.

label: Strawberry Leaf scorch
description: [DRAFT] Leaf scorch of strawberry is caused by the fungus Diplocarpon earlianum. It produces small purple spots on leaves that enlarge and merge, giving the foliage a scorched appearance.

label: Strawberry healthy
description: No disease

label: Tomato Bacterial spot
description: [DRAFT] Bacterial spot of tomato is caused by Xanthomonas species. It produces small, water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit that turn brown and scabby over time.

label: Tomato Early blight
description: [DRAFT] Early blight of tomato is caused by the fungus Alternaria solani. It produces target-spot lesions with concentric rings, typically starting on lower, older leaves.

label: Tomato Late blight
description: [DRAFT] Late blight of tomato is caused by the oomycete Phytophthora infestans, the same pathogen that causes potato late blight. It produces rapidly expanding water-soaked lesions that can collapse the whole plant in humid weather.

label: Tomato Leaf Mold
description: [DRAFT] Leaf mold of tomato is caused by the fungus Passalora fulva. It produces pale green to yellow spots on the upper leaf surface with an olive-green to grayish-purple velvety mold on the underside, common in humid greenhouse conditions.

label: Tomato Septoria leaf spot
description: [DRAFT] Septoria leaf spot of tomato is caused by the fungus Septoria lycopersici. It produces small, circular spots with dark borders and tan centers dotted with tiny black fruiting bodies, usually starting on lower leaves.

label: Tomato Two-spotted spider mite
description: [DRAFT] Two-spotted spider mite (Tetranychus urticae) is a tiny arachnid pest, not a fungus or bacterium. Feeding causes stippled yellow speckling on leaves, with fine webbing visible under heavy infestations.

label: Tomato Target Spot
description: [DRAFT] Target spot of tomato is caused by the fungus Corynespora cassiicola. It produces brown lesions with concentric rings, similar in appearance to early blight, affecting leaves, stems, and fruit.

label: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
description: [DRAFT] Tomato yellow leaf curl virus is a whitefly-transmitted geminivirus. It causes upward curling and yellowing of leaflet margins, stunted growth, and significant yield loss.

label: Tomato mosaic virus
description: [DRAFT] Tomato mosaic virus is a tobamovirus that causes mottled light and dark green mosaic patterns on leaves, leaf distortion, and stunting. It spreads easily through mechanical contact via contaminated tools or hands.

label: Tomato healthy
description: No disease
